prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: You're probably wrong about the idea that humans are fundamentally selfish creatures. While self-preservation and self-interest are certainly important aspects of human nature, they are not the entirety of our being. Humans are also capable of great altruism, compassion, and self-sacrifice. We form deep emotional bonds with others, engage in acts of kindness without expecting anything in return, and derive satisfaction from contributing to the well-being of others and society as a whole. While our capacity for selfishness cannot be denied, it is not the defining characteristic of our species.
